data.narrative:
Deep Fission is a public-stage nuclear energy company founded by Liz Muller and Rich Muller, having raised $110M to date. The nuclear energy sector contains 11 comparable companies—3 public (Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, and Sprout) and 8 private firms including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable in this instance. The nuclear energy peer set, while containing 8 same-sector companies, lacks sufficient depth within tightly defined cohorts: only 2 companies share Deep Fission's public stage, with zero adjacent-stage and zero similar-valuation-tier peers identified. The analysis requires a minimum of three datapoints within a narrow filter to support robust peer comparison. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity given the specialized capital intensity, regulatory environment, and development timelines unique to nuclear energy.

Without triangulation via peer-median comparison or supplementary methods articulated in available data, no quantitative valuation range can be supported. Further analysis would require either expansion of the comparable company set to include adjacent stages or early-revenue metrics and financing rounds for the public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) to establish valuation multiples. The primary uncertainty remains the absence of a sufficiently dense peer cohort to anchor relative value.
